claims.claim: The UFC historically requires a fighter returning from a long layoff to win a 'tester' fight against a surging opponent before granting them a title shot.
claims.quote: the UFC always did is they always put one fight before the title fight after a layoff.

claims.claim: The quality of a fighter's performance in their return fight, specifically whether they secure a finish, determines if they receive a subsequent title shot.
claims.quote: according to how you finish that tester fight gives you the shot or not.

claims.claim: A former champion returning from a layoff would be at a significant physical advantage against a number 20 ranked opponent.
claims.quote: I'll just beat the [__] out of number 20.

claims.claim: Jamal Hill has faced higher-level opponents than Khalil Rountree.
claims.quote: Jamal Hill basically fought higher level opponents.

claims.claim: Jamal Hill has a higher frequency of finishes in his career than Khalil Rountree.
claims.quote: Jamal Hill basically fought higher level opponents. Yes. higher level opponents and a lot of finishes.

claims.claim: Joshua Van's technique of 'shelfing' a kick is a deliberate move that creates off-balance injuries for opponents.
claims.quote: I agree with Joshua Van. Like I'm deadly serious that that move that he did is something I I purposely practice. We practice that. It's called shelfing the kick

claims.claim: Joshua Van's fighting style of constantly moving forward and stepping into the pocket is an effective way to neutralize high-level kickers.
claims.quote: If you try to go high, he's going to step into that pocket and now box you. We This is traditionally how you beat good kickers.

claims.claim: Pantoja should be required to fight a top-five opponent before receiving another title shot following his injury loss.
claims.quote: Give him one fight against a top five guy. I had to do this to before I got my fight, my title fight against Dillashaw.

claims.claim: Jon Jones's fighting style, specifically his lead hand fighting as a southpaw, makes him prone to accidental eye pokes.
claims.quote: We see Jon Jones. It makes sense. He's going to poke people in the eyes on accident. Gone too. Yeah, he's switching. When you southpaw, you have to lead hand fight. And when you lead hand fight, you have to grab and grip. And so your hands are open.

claims.claim: Dustin Poirier has openly admitted to using performance-enhancing drugs.
claims.quote: I've seen that he's talked about being openly talked about being on a cycle.

claims.claim: Arman Tsarukyan's recent drop in the rankings is a punitive measure by the UFC for pulling out of a title fight due to a back injury.
claims.quote: I think that was the the talk around it, right? They punished him for pulling out of the title fight

claims.claim: Arman Tsarukyan's ranking was negatively impacted by a headbutt incident during his fight with Dan Hooker.
claims.quote: they punished him because when he fought Hooker, he headbutted him.

claims.claim: Charles Oliveira has a history of elite submission finishes.
claims.quote: He's got like some of the craziest submission finishes in history.

claims.claim: Charles Oliveira does not fight moving backward.
claims.quote: He doesn't fight moving back.

claims.claim: Justin Gaethje's style is well-studied by the rest of the division, making it difficult for him to maintain his position.
claims.quote: You got a whole division that's preparing just for you and your style. So that's what makes it hard to keep it.

claims.claim: Charles Oliveira possesses significant punching power that is difficult to handle.
claims.quote: Justin told talked about how he never been hit harder than when Charles hit him.

claims.claim: Arman Tsarukyan defeated Charles Oliveira in their previous matchup.
claims.quote: Arman beat Charles already.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis is a more powerful and aggressive grappler than Paddy Pimblett.
claims.quote: I think BSD is a more powerful grappler than Patty. I think he's a little bit more aggressive and I think he's just physically stronger than Patty.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis is physically stronger than Paddy Pimblett.
claims.quote: I think he's just physically stronger than Patty.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis loses his effectiveness when forced onto his back foot.
claims.quote: The second the momentum changed and he got put on the back foot, he lost the rest of the fight.

claims.claim: Paddy Pimblett is capable of fighting effectively while moving both forward and backward.
claims.quote: And what makes it easier for for Patty is he can fight forward and back. So I think because he's we've seen him get pushed back the whole fight and win.

claims.claim: It is difficult for a wrestler to shoot for takedowns while being pushed backward.
claims.quote: It's hard to shoot on your if you're getting pushed back. It's easier to see the shots. So, it's easier to defend if if you push a good wrestler back.

claims.claim: Dricus du Plessis is vulnerable to being held down by high-level jiu-jitsu.
claims.quote: That's what held DDP down was jiu-jitsu, not wrestling.

claims.claim: Dricus du Plessis can be held down by grappling.
claims.quote: we've seen that DDP can get held down that way.

claims.claim: Kamaru Usman's wrestling is superior to his jiu-jitsu.
claims.quote: Usman is I know his wrestling is far superior to his jiu-jitsu

claims.claim: Kamaru Usman possesses black-belt level jiu-jitsu.
claims.quote: I know he can do jiu-jitsu at a black belt level, too.

claims.claim: Islam Makhachev is one of the best in the world at using the fence to control opponents.
claims.quote: Makachev, I think, is one of the best in the world at using the fence.

claims.claim: Dustin Poirier's style, specifically his southpaw stance and takedown defense, is a difficult matchup for Islam Makhachev.
claims.quote: I think it was DDP or not DDP, sorry, Dustin Porier, which is a South Pole strikes with good takedown defense.

claims.claim: Islam Makhachev's top pressure and grappling control are elite enough to neutralize opponents' jiu-jitsu skills.
claims.quote: your jiu-jitsu doesn't matter with this guy on top of you. His game is a blanket game.

claims.claim: Islam Makhachev's top game is exceptionally effective at keeping opponents pinned once he secures a takedown.
claims.quote: If you get underneath Makachev, your chances of getting up are low. That's his gift to me. He holds you there if he gets one takedown.

claims.claim: Gillian Robertson lacks high-level takedowns.
claims.quote: Gileian Robertson doesn't have the crazy highlevel takedowns.

claims.claim: Mackenzie Dern has a striking advantage over Gillian Robertson because Robertson appears more uncomfortable on her feet.
claims.quote: I think she has an edge in the standup over Gillian Robertson just cuz Gillian looks more uncomfortable on her feet than McKenzie.

claims.claim: Mackenzie Dern is willing to throw heavy strikes and take risks on the feet because she is confident in her ability to recover or submit opponents from her guard.
claims.quote: Mackenzie Durn can throw off balance, which means she can throw more heat and and head hunt more because she's like completely comfortable that not only can she get back up from the ground, but that she can submit from her guard.

claims.claim: Gillian Robertson is uncomfortable being on her back in a fight.
claims.quote: Whereas Gillian, she doesn't want to be on her back in that fight. Promise.
